key_event.1819-1828: Gran Colombia founded amid independence wars, fragile central authority
key_event.1829-1838: Dissolution of Gran Colombia; decentralized New Granada under Santander
key_event.1839-1848: War of the Supremes, 1843 centralist charter, Democratic Societies emerge
key_event.1849-1858: Federalist decentralization and liberal reforms weaken the center
key_event.1859-1868: 1863 Rionegro Constitution: radical federalism hollows out central state
key_event.1869-1878: DOIP secular education reform and 1876–77 "War of the Schools"
key_event.1889-1898: Regeneración centralism excludes and radicalizes Liberal opposition
key_event.1899-1908: War of a Thousand Days and Panama's secession
key_event.1909-1918: Reyes's fall and 1910 constitutional reform constraining the executive
key_event.1919-1928: Kemmerer Mission, Banco de la República; Banana Massacre
key_event.1929-1938: End of Conservative Hegemony; López's "Revolución en Marcha" reforms
key_event.1939-1948: Bogotazo assassination of Gaitán triggers La Violencia
key_event.1949-1958: La Violencia and the partisan-military convulsions
key_event.1959-1968: National Front pact; Marquetalia assault births FARC/ELN insurgency
key_event.1969-1978: 1977 National Civic Strike amid Paper Leviathan and rising insurgency
key_event.1979-1988: Cartels, guerrillas, and paramilitaries shatter state's violence monopoly
key_event.1989-1998: Absent Leviathan losing ground to guerrillas, paras, drug economy
key_event.1999-2008: Plan Colombia and Uribe's Democratic Security state-building
key_event.2009-2018: FARC peace accord and Victims' Law mobilizing civil society
key_event.2019-2028: Mass national strikes and Petro's election amid uneven state reach
